---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: middle, slideblue

.center[
.size55[
**Issues in Environmental Policy (PLSC 323)**
]]

<br>

.size45[**Today's Agenda**

Reviewing our plan for the semester
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Bring a few "Get out of jail" free cards

<br>

Welcome to PLSC 323: Issues in Environmental Policy

IS EVERYBODY IN THE RIGHT PLACE?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[**Designing Solutions for Environmental Problems**]

<br>

### Required Courses
.size35[
+ BIOL 163 Science of the Environment

+ ECON 225 Introduction to Environmental Economics

+ PHIL 320 Environmental Ethics

+ PLSC 323 Issues in Environmental Policy
]

???

Also let me welcome you to the capstone for the "Designing Solutions for Environmental Problems" fusion certificate.

HOW MANY PEOPLE ARE HERE FOR THIS?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.size80[**Introductions**]

.size55[
1. Name

2. Year in school

3. Major

4. Certificate courses completed?
]

???

Let's go around the room for introductions.
- I'll go first.

1. I'm Dr. Justin Leinaweaver.

2. FSU undergrad, masters in IR at UCD and PhD at Trinity.
  + My ninth year at Drury

3. I'm a political scientist
  + Research interests: international politics, environmental politics and bargaining / negotiations.

<br>

YOUR TURN:
1) NAME,
2) YEAR, and
3) MAJOR



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/02-2-politics1.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

???

Given our large number of non-majors we should take a moment to define a key concept in our class: Politics

WHEN YOU THINK OF THE WORD "POLITICAL" WHAT DO YOU THINK OF?

*DISCUSS*

<br>

Ok, let me see if I can help you get to how political scientists think about politics.

Let's play a game.

Here's the deal: As a class you have to distribute a couple of index cards.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/01-1-get_out_of_jail_free.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

???

Each of these is a "get out of jail free" card.

You can use one of these to help yourself out of a jam in class this semester.

- Miss a paper deadline? Add two days!
- Miss a class? Get an excused absence!

Exceptions?

- Class policies, not university policies (e.g. plagiarism) or laws (e.g. no murder)
- Can't apply to the final exam

I'll hang onto the cards up here and you tell me who to give them to.

**That's it, that's the game. Go!**

<br>

# Notes on the game FOR YOU
+ Class must distribute X "Get out of jail free" cards in ten minutes

+ Each card is worth a free excused absence OR a two day extension to a paper deadline (not the final)

+ Number of cards: Approx 25% of the class?

+ You keep all of the "get out of jail free" cards in hand until the end of the game. No one can just claim them by walking up and taking it. Students must make an affirmative case why they should get one. I like this! A literal government hand out!




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/02-2-politics1.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

.size40[.center[.content-box-red[**What just happened?**]]]

.size40[.center[.content-box-red[**What was your experience of the game?**]]]

???

Ok, everybody take two minutes on your own to reflect on our game.

Write down a few notes: How did you approach this game? What was your strategy? Why?

Jot down some notes you could use to explain what just happened.

<br>

OK, WHAT JUST HAPPENED?

- How was it played?
- What was your experience?
- Did others play it differently?
- What was the outcome and why did we reach that end?

<br>

Now, assume for the moment that our game was a useful simulation of "politics."

Take your notes, take two more minutes and write me a short definition of politics.

Alright, let's hear what you came up with!

*PRESENT and DISCUSS*

<br>

Games and simulations are often a useful tool for exploring politics.

What we just played was a fairly basic distribution game.

It highlights a ton of important stuff, but we'll just focus on the big idea for now.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/02-2-politics1.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

.size40[.right[.content-box-blue[**Decision-making in a community**]]]

.size40[.right[.content-box-blue[**How we make and enforce "the rules"**]]]

.size40[.right[.content-box-blue[**Who gets what, when and how?**]]]

???

In various textbooks you'll see politics defined as:

- Who gets what, when and how?
- Decision-making that affects a community
- How we make the rules and whether or not they matter

In essence, anytime a group of people have to come together to make a decision, a political scientist can be found who is interested in the process.

ANY QUESTIONS ON THIS BROAD DEFINITION OF THE POLITICS CONCEPT?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/01-1-env_pols.jpg')
background-size: 50%
background-position: right
class: slidegreen

.pull-left[
.size50[
.center[
**What's going on in the world of environmental politics?**
]
+ Global

+ National

+ Local
]]

???

That in mind, what's going on in the world of environmental problems / politics?

That definition in mind, open discussion: What's going on these days in terms of environmental politics / problems / challenges?

*ON BOARD* Cols: Local, National, Global

<br>

Similarities and differences across the columns?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

# LO1: Content Knowledge
.size55[
Students should demonstrate the ability to organize the investigation of an environmental problem in terms of its key stakeholders, institutions and uncertainties involved.
]

???



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

# LO2: Critical Thinking and Problem-Solving Skills
.size50[
Students should demonstrate policy-based problem-solving skills by designing plans that balance the interests of stakeholders against the constraints of established institutions and uncertainty.

]

???



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

# LO3: Communication Skills
.size55[
Students should demonstrate the ability to communicate their policy proposals in clear, concise ways that are tailored to the relevant audiences.
]

???



---

class: middle, slideblue

# Semester in Three Sections 

.size45[
1. How do we approach environmental politics? (4 wks)

2. What are the complicating factors in environmental policymaking? (5 wks)

3. Designing an environmental policy (6 wks)
]




---

class: middle, center

.size55[**Course Grade**]

<br>

```{r}
tibble(
  col1 = c("Participation", "Paper 1: The Problem", "Paper 2: Complicating Factors", "Paper 3: Policy Design", "Total"),
  col2 = c("", "(Feb 18)", "(Apr 1)", "(Final)", ""),
  col3 = c(40, rep(20, 3), 100)
) |>
  kableExtra::kbl(align = c("l", "c", "c"), col.names = c("", "", "%")) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(font_size = 40) |>
  kableExtra::column_spec(1, width = "20em") |>
  kableExtra::column_spec(2, width = "7em") |>
  kableExtra::row_spec(c(0, 5), bold = TRUE, background = "#b3ccff")
```

???

Class is a seminar designed around helping you tackle a project rooted in real world problem solving

Each of you will select a local environmental problem, analyze the complicating factors and then propose a policy to address it.


---

class: middle, slideblue

.size60[**Participation**]

.size50[
+ Get to class on time,

+ Have the materials you need to be productive,

+ Be engaged while in class (e.g., no sleeping, playing with your gadgets, leaving early, arriving late).
]

???

Let's talk participation.

In order for me to ensure you make progress on our learning objectives I need you in class and playing along.

This class is a seminar, not a lecture.

I want you to be a well informed citizen of the world and that means taking ownership of the process.

Therefore, I have made participation worth 20% of the final grade, AND

I expect you to earn a participation point in every class.

How?
1. Do the reading,

2. Bring the reading, and

3. Be actively engaged in class (no sleeping, playing with your gadgets, leaving early, arriving late).

QUESTIONS?

These are basically free points for everybody.

The two most common ways people lose these points?

- Come to class late, or 

- come to class without the assignment completed for the day



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/01_1-coyote.jpg')
background-size: 85%
background-position: center

???

The only way I can ensure everyone makes progress on the LOs is by being in class and doing the work.

This means I have to make sure you are here!

So, this class has an attendance cliff.

If you have more than five unexcused absences during the semester you will not be able to earn greater than a C+.

Regardless of assignment grades.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/01_1-office_space.jpg')
background-size: 65%
background-position: center

???

**Excused absence coming up?**

Illness? Sports?

Let me know **ahead of time** and I'll give you an assignment to complete to earn your lost participation points. 

Easy peasy.

<br>

Here's the other part of this deal.

Covid is still a GIANT pain in the ass and life always gets hectic during the semester.

Keep me in the loop and I will have your back!

I am not here to make your life harder.

I genuinely want you to learn this material.

So, if things are getting rough for you please come talk to me.

**I will always offer you flexibility in the face of struggle IF YOU COME TO ME BEFORE DEADLINES HAVE PASSED!**

SOUND FAIR?

<br>

ANY QUESTIONS ON ATTENDANCE AND PARTICIPATION?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/01_1-matrix_meme.jpg')
background-size: 95%
background-position: center

???

Your first job is to download the syllabus from Moodle and read it carefully.

It includes detail on everything you need to know.
- Assignment deadlines
- Readings
- Drury policies

ALSO, I can see who downloaded it, so don’t delay!

<br>

We'll be using Moodle for submitting assignments and sharing readings.

Let me know if you have trouble using our Moodle site.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/01-1-ozarks_forest.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: bottom

.right[.textwhite[.size60[**Why is "the wilderness" a serious problem for society?**]]]

???

For Friday, read the article by William Cronon on the trouble with wilderness.

I love this article.
